rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, January 9, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1, Defendant Hanson Permanente Cement Inc.¿s motion to compel plaintiffs¿ further responses to its special interrogatories and form interrogatories and request for sanctions of $780 is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JOSE ALCAINE et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs' Further Responses To Its Special Interrogatories (Set One) And Judicial Council Form Interrogatories (Set One) And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, January 9, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1, Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.¿s motion to compel plaintiffs¿ further responses to its special interrogatories and form interrogatories and request for sanctions of $780 is granted. No opposition filed. Plaintiffs must provide verified responses within 10 days. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m., not 9:00a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ELROY PORCHIA et al VS. 3M COMPANY, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR IN INTER et al

rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, January 9, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2, Defendant Oakfabco, Inc.¿s motion to compel plaintiffs¿ further responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories and inspection demands is granted. No opposition filed. Plaintiffs must provide verified responses within 10 days. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m., not 9:00a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
